script:
Pickerington Parents, Millennials, and Gen X -

School board elections matter for kids in OUR neighborhoods!

It’s time for us to match the energy of the generation before us. In the last Pickerington school board election, 48% of voters over 60 turned out. Only 16% of us did. They care deeply about this community’s future, and they show it by voting.

Now it’s our turn to do the same. Our kids are in these classrooms. 

Let’s make sure our voices are heard, too, and join Pickerington’s teachers and school staff in supporting:

Ryan Holstine
Brooke LaGrasso
Charles Newman 

Vote November 4th in the Pickerington School Board election!

Paid for by New Leadership Pickerington. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee.

script:
I'm Carol Greenstreet, running for Woodland Park Re-2 School Board, District E. I believe strong schools are vital to a strong community. I'm committed to responsible budgeting, transparent operation, vibrant community partnerships and a positive district culture. My vision is simple: I want all schools and students to thrive. 

I will prioritize: 
Transparent fiscal reporting
Academic rigor and career pathways
Student and teacher retention
Community partnerships
Extracurricular programming
Safe, reliable facilities

As a 20 year resident with previous school board experience, I know what our community wants and how to represent it well. I am experienced in laying a strong foundation on which to build a future for our schools, for our community's kids. 

Every vote counts! I appreciate your support on Nov. 4th!

script:
Hello, my name is Melissa Reed, your candidate for Ketchikan Gateway Borough School Board 3-year seat. As someone who's already serving on the board, I understand the needs of our community, and I am committed to continuing to work hard for our children and staff.

With Election Day on October 7, 2025, I wanted to take a moment to thank you for considering my candidacy. As a servant leader, I've worked to bring transparency and a commitment to providing the best for our kids to the board. I'm proud of what we've achieved so far, continuing to look to the future and find a supportive, understanding permanent superintendent while prioritizing Life and Safety for our children.


When I'm not working to improve our schools, you can find me outside on a local beach or baking up a storm at home. I'm dedicated to making our schools the best and I ask for your vote on October 7.

Reply stop to opt-out. Paid for by Melissa Reed for KGBSD.

If this is the first time you’re hearing about me, there’s still time to learn more about my plan to build a safer, smarter, and more resilient Haverhill. My priorities are clear:
✅ Creating a stronger budget through smarter tax policy
✅ Curbing overdevelopment while fixing existing infrastructure
✅ Improving our schools and supporting teachers and families
✅ Restoring transparency and accountability at City Hall

Haverhill deserves leadership that listens, shows up, and delivers real results. Together, we can get our city back on track and build a future we’re proud to call home.

Learn more at www.brandon4haverhill.com
Follow on Instagram: @brandon4haverhill
Facebook: Brandon Burns for Mayor of Haverhill

Let’s make history, vote Brandon Burns for Mayor tomorrow and let’s bring leadership back to City Hall.

Hi - this is Mubarek Ahmed and I am running for Dearborn city council. The city deserves leadership that listens, solves problems, and puts people first. Here's a quick look at my priorities:

If these values matter to you, I’d be grateful to be one of your seven votes for city council. To make sure your voice is heard, check your voter registration at Michigan.gov/Vote.

Since 2010 Hartwell's population grew by 289 people while Hart County grew by over 3,600. Don't Drink the Kool-Aid.

Our homeowner taxes are going down, we just provided Homestead Protection for our citizens, our utility rates are the lowest in the region, our water and sewer plants have been updated for our future, financially we are stronger than ever. 

This election is important, I love Hartwell and I appreciate that you have trusted me for 20 years. Now we need for you to show up and Vote for Tray Hicks. Let's Keep Hartwell the Best City by a Dam Site.
If you have questions or concerns call or text me at 706-436-9695.

My name is Jeremy Ruse, I'm running for the Olympia School Board Director position, District Five. We're a couple days away from the August 5th Primary, I'm hoping for the opportunity to earn your vote.

I've been a member of this community for over 35 years, I've raised four kids, built a business, our roots are deeply set in Olympia. I'm grateful for the support and opportunities I've experienced, I want to give back. I believe our youth are our greatest opportunity as a community, I believe I have the experience and perspective to help shape their future, I'm hoping you'll give me the opportunity to do so.

The school board is an incredibly important position, the community entrusts board members to guide policy, and be fiscally responsible with tax payers dollars. If given the opportunity, I will do so in a professional manner. I will work with the administration to assure our students learn in a safe and accepting environment, my business background has prepared me to tackle the budget, it's my intent to work toward a long-term, sustainable budget. Being fiscally responsible provides our district the freedom to focus on students, and the confidence of the community that we have their best interest at heart. I'm confident I have the tools, resources, and experience to be successful. If elected, I will work diligently to represent the community, with transparency. 

Please let me know if I can answer your questions, your vote counts and I hope I've earned it.

script:
Hey, it’s Oscar Alejandro, your candidate for Miami City Commissioner of District 3. This isn’t an automated message; I’m personally texting you because that’s how this grassroots campaign operates.

As a veteran, I came back home to Miami to continue serving the community that raised me. I’m the only candidate running in opposition to the MAGA regime and the corruption that defines it. I spoke out against the 287(g) partnership between Miami PD and ICE, against canceling this year’s election without a voter referendum, and have made it clear that I will not support any partisan agenda that prioritizes the opinions of outside influences over the people of Miami.

My priorities are straightforward: addressing the housing crisis, promoting civic engagement, especially amongst younger people, and establishing a foundational baseline of ethical governance that can be built upon to both address issues at hand without bureaucratic excuses and provide residents with the resources necessary to ensure that their voices can be heard regardless of economic or social status.

The people of Miami deserve a candidate who’s not the lesser of two evils, but instead, a leader who understands the burdens of working-class families because they themselves have lived it. 

VBM ballots have already been mailed out, and you have until 10/23 to request yours before the election on 11/04. This number is your direct line to ask me more about my campaign, voter information, and anything else. Let’s take Miami back for working-class families, together!

script:
Hi, I’m Sheniqua Freeman, your candidate for Fort Valley City Council – East Ward. With less a month until Election Day on November 4th, 2025, I want to take a moment to share my vision for our community.

As a dedicated homemaker, community leader, and proud graduate of Fort Valley State University, I’ve developed a deep understanding of our city’s challenges and its potential. My time on the Board of Directors at Macon Little Theatre, where I made history as the first African American member and later served as Chair, strengthened my belief in the power of collaboration, creativity, and community engagement.

I’m passionate about tackling the issues that matter most to our residents — from improving neighborhood safety to encouraging homeownership and enhancing our quality of life. By being committed to being a spirit of reconciliation and responsive dedication to working tirelessly to make Fort Valley a thriving and connected city for all.

Early voting begins October 14th and runs through October 31st, so make your plan to vote early and avoid the lines on Election Day. Every vote counts on November 4th, 2025 — and together, we can build the Fort Valley we all deserve. NO TIME TO DILLY DALLY VOTE FREEMAN FOR FORT VALLEY!

script:
Karl Wiggins here, your candidate for Northport City Council, District 3, and I have an important message for you as we approach election day.
Last Saturday, we celebrated the grand opening of our new, amazing River Run Park sports complex, and on Monday we announced that RRP will host the HBCU Athletic Conference Baseball and Softball championship tournaments for the next three years, starting in April 2026. This is only the first of many, many opportunities for out-of-town guests to experience this beautiful facility, as well as our local restaurants, hotels, shops, and businesses. Northport is a growing city and I am excited to have played a part in this success.
Unfortunately, there is a powerful and vocal group that does not want to see growth and progress like this. They are using money and social media to drum up negativity and hostility because they don’t want to see Northport grow and thrive. They like things the way that they used to be. Everyone is entitled to their own opinion, but I strongly disagree. I want to see more retail opportunities, more houses, more restaurants, more jobs, and more money flow into our community. If you want to see that too, can I count on your vote on August 26th?

Visit this link to watch a 1-minute video on a few things that the current city council and I have accomplished in the past two and a half years: https://wiggins4northport.net/2025/08/17/what-has-this-northport-city-council-accomplished/

Reach me at wiggins4northport@gmail.com or reply here.

script:
I'm Rob Taylor, candidate for Saratoga Springs City Council.

As you consider your vote for Saratoga Springs City Council, I ask that you remember that as a seasoned finance expert with 30 years in banking, I have the experience and education to ensure our city's financial health and make informed decisions on complex budgets and other issues.   

I will bring to Saratoga Springs City Council common sense leadership with real solutions, not gridlock; open spaces, not pavement; and smart, not uncontrolled growth.   

REMEMBER, YOUR VOTE IS YOUR VOICE. It’s likely too late to mail your ballot because of a change in Utah election laws; postmarks are no longer sufficient for a ballot to be counted. HOWEVER, you can still drop off your ballot before 8:00 p.m. this evening at the Saratoga Springs Elections Drop Box located at 1307 Commerce Dr, Saratoga Springs, UT 84045 or the Saratoga Springs Vote Center located at 612 Pony Express Parkway, Saratoga Springs, UT 84045.   

Every vote counts, and I'm honored to have your support. Thank you for considering my candidacy.

script:
Hi, it’s Lance. Ballots are hitting mailboxes this week! As your neighbor, I’m focused on building safe routes so kids can get to school and parks without worry, and on investing in sidewalks, streets, and neighborhood infrastructure that keeps Traverse City strong. By voting early and returning your ballot, you’ll help make sure our city grows responsibly while protecting the character that makes TC home. 

Learn more: https://votelance.com/

I’m running for City Commission because Traverse City is more than where my family lives and works – it’s our HOME, and I care deeply about our city’s future.

HOME is also a great acronym for where my focus is:
Housing that’s attainable for people who live and work here.
Opportunities for year-round economic and business growth.
Mobility options that are safe and accessible for everyone.
Environmental decisions that protect what makes TC special.
